RFP document (carrying evaluation criteria) expected 2026-06-20, now 11 days overdue. No discovery call to surface buyer priorities.
No champion. Only contact is Jean-François Winkler (BuyIn intermediary, not Deutsche Telekom). Buyer defers meetings to post-shortlist.

metrics.narrative: No business problem, quantified impact, or qualitative pain has been articulated by the relevant stakeholder deal is a partner-intermediated RFP motion run through BuyIn (the Orange and Deutsche Telekom procurement joint venture). The only Salesforce field referencing value is Products (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement), which names the solution scope but not the buyer's pain or ROI case. The initial phone call with the prospect and Monika Mutscher (source: Next Step 2026-07-01) has not yet been followed by discovery with the owner or the broader Zycus team. No Avoma calls with substantive content (one 2025-06-25 touchpoint, not recorded). The RFP document, when received, may carry the buyer's requirements and evaluation criteria, but as of 2026-07-01 it is 11 days overdue and Zycus has no direct line to the Deutsche Telekom business sponsor to qualify pain. The recommended move is to secure the RFP and, once shortlisted, immediately initiate pain and ROI discovery with the Deutsche Telekom procurement and IT leads.

sources: living memory: buyer defers meetings to post-shortlist
champion.narrative: No champion identified. The only active Zycus contact is Jean-François Winkler (BuyIn intermediary, Evaluator role), who is not a Deutsche Telekom employee and does not have access to the Deutsche Telekom Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or business sponsor (source: SF OpportunityContactRole). The buyer stated they will meet with vendors only after RFP submission and shortlisting (source: living memory, prior sweep), so Zycus has had no opportunity to build a champion relationship. The two Deutsche Telekom Decision Makers on the opportunity (Özlem Seidler, Carsten Nazet) and the Evaluator (Guevenc Oezer) have not engaged. Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is not populated. The initial phone call between the prospect and Monika Mutscher (source: Next Step 2026-07-01) has not yet been followed by a broader Zycus team introduction or a champion-building session. This is a critical risk: without a champion, Zycus cannot influence the RFP scoring, counter competitor positioning, or secure a preference signal before shortlisting. The recommended move is to secure the RFP and, once shortlisted, to identify and develop a champion among the Deutsche Telekom procurement or IT leads who will advocate for Zycus through the evaluation.

competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Salesforce field Competitors__c is not populated, and no Avoma call, Next Step, or Task names a rival. The RFP motion itself implies a multi-vendor evaluation, but Zycus has no visibility into the shortlist, the incumbent (if any), or the competitors' positioning. Salesforce fields How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c are not populated. The partner-intermediated RFP process (BuyIn) blocks direct competitive intelligence from the buyer. This is a blind bid: Zycus cannot differentiate or counter competitor narratives without knowing who is in the eval and what they are saying. The recommended move is to ask the BuyIn intermediary (or, once shortlisted, the Deutsche Telekom procurement lead) who else is bidding, what the incumbent is (if any), and what the buyer's stated concerns are with the current solution or the other vendors.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is unidentified. No Deutsche Telekom stakeholder has articulated an operational or financial problem that iContract, iSource, iSupplier, or eProcurement would solve. The only evidence of buyer intent is the RFP motion itself (source: Next Step 2026-07-01), which implies a procurement need but does not name the pain owner, the current-state problem, or the urgency. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c are not populated. The initial phone call between the prospect and Monika Mutscher has not yet been followed by a discovery session with the owner or the broader Zycus team (source: Next Step 2026-07-01). The one recorded Avoma touchpoint (2025-06-25, not recorded) predates the current RFP cycle by over a year and carries no content. This is a blind RFP bid: Zycus cannot anchor the response to a quantified pain or build a champion without knowing what the buyer is trying to fix. The recommended move is to secure the RFP and, once shortlisted, to run a pain-discovery session with the Deutsche Telekom procurement and IT leads, asking: what is broken today, what is the cost of inaction, who owns the problem.

paper_process.narrative: The paper process is partially visible. A three-party NDA (BuyIn/Orange/DT, Deutsche Telekom, Zycus) has been completed and signed by all parties as of 2026-07-01 (source: Next Step 2026-07-01). This is the first formal contracting step. No information on MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or ERP integration scope is available. Salesforce field Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated, so no incumbent contract forcing function is known. The RFP document, when received, may specify contracting requirements, but as of 2026-07-01 it is 11 days overdue. No SI or partner role in implementation is documented beyond BuyIn's intermediary role in the RFP process. The recommended move is to ask the BuyIn intermediary (or, once shortlisted, the Deutsche Telekom procurement lead) about the contracting timeline, legal review steps, and any InfoSec or integration requirements that will gate the close.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. Salesforce lists Özlem Seidler (Category & Stakeholder Management IT, telekom.de) and Carsten Nazet (Head of BSS-OSS Sourcing, BuyIn) as Decision Maker contact roles, but neither has engaged with Zycus to date. The buyer stated they will meet only after RFP submission and shortlisting (source: living memory, prior sweep), deferring all EB discovery and validation to a post-shortlist phase. This is a critical access risk: if Zycus is not shortlisted, there will be no opportunity to map the EB, validate budget authority, or secure sponsorship. The only active contact is Jean-François Winkler (BuyIn intermediary, Evaluator role), who is not the budget authority. No Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) carries EB information. The recommended move is to demand EB access as a condition of RFP response quality, or at minimum to map the EB through the BuyIn intermediary before shortlisting.

sources: living memory: buyer defers meetings to post-shortlist
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially mapped. The buyer is running a formal RFP through BuyIn (the Orange and Deutsche Telekom procurement joint venture), with an NDA signed by all three parties (BuyIn/Orange/DT, Deutsche Telekom, Zycus) as of 2026-07-01 (source: Next Step 2026-07-01). The RFP was expected 2026-06-20 and is now 11 days overdue. The buyer stated they will meet with vendors only after RFP submission and shortlisting (source: living memory, prior sweep), which implies a staged process: (1) RFP issuance, (2) vendor response, (3) shortlisting, (4) vendor meetings and deeper evaluation, (5) selection. Salesforce field What_is_the_decision_process__c is not populated. The call sequence to date is thin: one 2025-06-25 touchpoint (not recorded), then 372-day silence, then NDA completion and rep outreach 2026-06-20. No Deutsche Telekom Decision Makers (Seidler, Nazet) or Evaluators (Oezer) have engaged. The approval chain (Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) is unmapped. The recommended move is to secure the RFP and, once shortlisted, to map the approval chain and timeline with the Deutsche Telekom procurement lead.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown. The RFP document, expected 2026-06-20 and now 11 days overdue, will carry the evaluation framework, but Zycus has not yet received it (source: Next Step 2026-07-01, rep follow-up sent 2026-06-20 with no reply). No discovery call with Deutsche Telekom has taken place to surface their evaluation priorities, must-haves, or scoring model. The only recorded engagement is an initial phone call between the prospect and Monika Mutscher, with the rest of Zycus and owner Dirk Fischbach yet to be introduced (source: Next Step 2026-07-01). Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are not populated. This is a blind RFP bid: Zycus cannot tailor the response or differentiate without knowing what Deutsche Telekom values most. The recommended move is to secure the RFP immediately and, once shortlisted, to run a discovery session with the Deutsche Telekom procurement and IT leads to validate the criteria and identify any unstated priorities.

Partner-intermediated RFP motion with deferred buyer access. BuyIn (Orange and Deutsche Telekom procurement joint venture) is running the RFP, and the buyer stated they will meet only after RFP submission and shortlisting. This defers pain discovery, EB/DM mapping, and champion-building to post-shortlist (high risk if not shortlisted). The winning move is to submit an RFP response that anticipates their pain (procurement cost reduction, supplier risk, compliance automation) and requests a discovery workshop as part of the shortlist evaluation, while in parallel researching Deutsche Telekom's current procurement stack to infer the incumbent and competitive set.

Multi-thread and executive access gap. Only one contact role on the opportunity (Jean-François Winkler, BuyIn intermediary, evaluator), zero Deutsche Telekom procurement, IT, or business sponsor contact roles. The recommended move is to request the names and titles of the Deutsche Telekom procurement lead, CFO sponsor, technical evaluators, and approval chain as part of the RFP clarification process, and to propose an executive briefing (owner's manager, John Woodcock, to the relevant stakeholder Procurement or CFO) as a post-shortlist deliverable.

RFP delay as early warning signal. RFP expected 20 June 2026, now 11 days overdue with no updated timeline from the buyer. A delayed RFP may signal internal buyer delays, scope changes, budget freeze, or procurement re-prioritization. The recommended move is to escalate the RFP follow-up (owner Dirk Fischbach to Jean-François Winkler and request a direct Deutsche Telekom procurement contact) within the next 7 days, and if no RFP is received within 21 days total (by ~11 July 2026), to propose a discovery call to validate the procurement is still active.
